FAA reports considered suspect

Larry Lipman The Palm Beach Post October 1994
Data & DatabasesInvestigative MethodsPublic Safety

This story delves into the difficulties of interpreting Federal Aviation Administration's service difficulty reports, highlighting the misleading nature of data, its inconsistencies, and how such analyses can impact public perception and reporting accuracy.

Sometimes the numbers look just too good to be true. That's when it pays to be suspicious... It turns out that the FAA admits the data from the airlines is neither consistent nor used as well as it might be for statistical analysis of either the airline's performance or safety trends.
